answer:
<think> The Corps area and the National Guard of the United States are part of the Reserve components of the military. The Reserve components are divided into two main categories: the Army National Guard and the Reserve components of the other branches. The Army National Guard is further divided into the Army Reserve and the Army National Guard, but the term "Corps area" is not typically used to describe the Army Reserve. Therefore, I will focus on the Army National Guard. The Army National Guard is a reserve component of the United States Army, and it is organized into a state-based system, with each state having its own National Guard. The National Guard is also referred to as the "Corps area" in some contexts, as it is a collective term for the National Guard units in a specific geographic area. Therefore, the Corps area and the National Guard of the United States are part of the Army National Guard. </think>
<answer> Army National Guard </answer>
